LAW AND MOTION, 302 THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - DENIED. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE WORD "DRAWEE" IN LABOR CODE SECTION 212(C) MEANS "THE BANK ON WHICH THE CHECK IS DRAWN" WESTON V. FED EX OFFICE AND PRINT SERVICES, INC. JANUARY 27, 2010_F SUPP. 2ND_(2010 WL 1641065) (CITING BLACK'S LAW DICTIONARY 495 (6TH ED. 1990); THE AMERICAN HERITAGE COLLEGE DICTIONARY, 419 (3D EDITION 1997). THEREFORE, SECTION 212(C) DOES APPLY TO THIS CASE BECAUSE THE DRAWEE IS A BANK, SPECIFICALLY BANK OF AMERICA. LABOR CODE SECTION 212(C) REQUIRES THAT, "THE INSTRUMENT SHALL BE NEGOTIABLE AND PAYABLE IN CASH, ON DEMAND, WITHOUT DISCOUNT, AT ANY PLACE OF BUSINESS OF THE DRAWEE CHOSEN BY THE PERSON ENTITLED TO ENFORCE THE INSTRUMENT." THE COURT FINDS THAT "ON DEMAND" MEANS "WHEN PRESENTED OR ON REQUEST FOR PAYMENT." BLACK'S LAW DICTIONARY 8TH ED. 2004.) "DISCOUNT" MEANS "A REDUCTION FROM THE FULL AMOUNT OR VALUE OF SOMETHING." ID. PLAINTIFF HAS RAISED TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT AS TO WHETHER THE CHECKS WERE PAYABLE "ON DEMAND" AND "WITHOUT DISCOUNT," AND TO THE ENTITLEMENT OF RESTITUTIONARY RELIEF. ORDER ON MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (DEFENDANT'S ORDER ON OBJECTIONS SIGNED; COPIES GIVEN TO COUNSEL PRIOR TO HEARING. COURT SUSTAINED ALL THREE OF PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS (NO ORDER GIVEN).) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Sep-02-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT THE WACKENHUT CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, ALTERNATIVELY, FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.

APPEARANCES: Eric M. Epstein, Esq. of Law Offices Eric M. Epstein 310/552-5366 & Jason Kuller, Esq. of Thierman Law Firm, P.C. 775/284-1500 (appearing via CourtCall) for pltf & opposing party; Calvin E. Davis, Esq. of Gordon & Rees LLP 213/576-5000 for deft & moving party.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - DENIED. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE WORD "DRAWEE" IN LABOR CODE SECTION 212(c) MEANS "THE BANK ON WHICH THE CHECK IS DRAWN" WESTON V. FED EX OFFICE AND PRINT SERVICES, INC. JANUARY 27, 2010_F SUPP. 2ND_(2010 WL 1641065) (CITING BLACK'S LAW DICTIONARY 495 (6TH ed. 1990); THE AMERICAN HERITAGE COLLEGE DICTIONARY, 419 (3D EDITION 1997). THEREFORE, SECTION 212(c) DOES APPLY TO THIS CASE BECAUSE THE DRAWEE IS A BANK, SPECIFICALLY BANK OF AMERICA. LABOR CODE SECTION 212(c) REQUIRES THAT, "THE INSTRUMENT SHALL BE NEGOTIABLE AND PAYABLE IN CASH, ON DEMAND, WITHOUT DISCOUNT, AT ANY PLACE OF BUSINESS OF THE DRAWEE CHOSEN BY THE PERSON ENTITLED TO ENFORCE THE INSTRUMENT." THE COURT FINDS THAT "ON DEMAND" MEANS "WHEN PRESENTED OR ON REQUEST FOR PAYMENT." BLACK'S LAW DICTIONARY 8TH ed. 2004.) "DISCOUNT" MEANS "A REDUCTION FROM THE FULL AMOUNT OR VALUE OF SOMETHING." Id. PLAINTIFF HAS RAISED TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT AS TO WHETHER THE CHECKS WERE PAYABLE "ON DEMAND" AND "WITHOUT DISCOUNT," AND TO THE ENTITLEMENT OF RESTITUTIONARY RELIEF. Order on motion for summary judgment signed in open court. (Defendant's order on objections signed; copies given to counsel prior to hearing. Court sustained all three of plaintiff's objections (no order given).) (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797
